search_queries_used: site:diabetesjournals.org 2026 diabetes technology CGM recommendations data review training skin reactions
site:dha.gov.ae continuous glucose monitoring guideline UAE
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic CGM target interpretation or insulin adjustment
device-brand, alarm, calibration or treatment instructions
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: CGM metrics require patient-specific goals and clinician interpretation.
No device selection, medicine, dose or therapy adjustment is generated.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: People with diabetes using CGM; age, pregnancy, treatment, caregiver role and device type require individual review.
setting_applicability: Diabetes technology review in outpatient or specialist care.
UAE_applicability: International ADA guidance transfers partially; UAE device approval, supply, insurance, training and prescribing policy governs.
recency_verification: ADA Standards of Care 2026 section 7 was reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: The 2026 Standards are current for the research date.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact ADA device-principles section supports interval change, patient concerns, caregiver and training context, previous correspondence and clinician discussion without selecting a CGM device or changing treatment.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documenting individual circumstances, preferences, caregiver role, device training, data sharing and clinician support needs.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact CGM section supports associated symptoms, relevant negatives, focused assessment including skin reactions, home-monitoring review, clinician-entered plan, safety-net discussion and follow-up only when documented; no target or therapy change is generated.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documenting CGM use, individual preference, continuity, periodic use, skin reaction, interfering-substance and reviewed-data context without changing therapy.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Blood-glucose monitoring backup and accuracy context were reviewed but no device or monitoring instruction is auto-generated.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documenting monitoring method, circumstances, treatment context, suspected inaccuracy, interfering substances and reviewed blood-glucose records.

unresolved_source_gaps: Device, wear time, data period, time in range, low or high patterns, alarms, accuracy, skin reaction, insulin regimen, interpretation and plan remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
